- Extensive aromatic signals (~25H total) suggesting multiple aromatic rings
- Aliphatic CH2 groups consistent with an amino acid side chain
- CF3 group (from formula)
- Two nitrogens and sulfur

The spectroscopic pattern is consistent with a **protected cysteine derivative**. The benzyl CH2 at 4.66 ppm and aromatic signals indicate Cbz (benzyloxycarbonyl) protection. The tBu singlet at 1.22 ppm indicates TBDMS (tert-butyldimethylsilyl) protection on the thiol.

The presence of CF3, two nitrogens, and six oxygens suggests this is likely **N-Cbz-S-TBDMS-L-cysteine with a TFA-protected or TFA-amidated amine**, or possibly a **protected cysteine derivative with additional aromatic substituents**.

The most straightforward interpretation of the core structure is N-Cbz-S-TBDMS-L-cysteine, though the complete molecular formula suggests additional modifications (possibly a TFA salt or additional protecting group on the amine).
